sourceTitle: Manage client work in a flexible project tracker

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using Notion as a single hub for client work?

options.text: It eliminates the need for any external communication tools like Slack or email.
options.explanation: Notion centralizes information, but you can still use communication tools; it doesn’t replace them entirely.

options.text: All project‑related information—briefs, notes, files, and status—can be stored and accessed in one place.
options.explanation: Correct. Notion acts as a single hub where you can connect briefs, meeting notes, client feedback, documents, and more.

options.text: It automatically generates invoices for every client project.
options.explanation: Invoice generation is not mentioned as a core feature of the project tracker.

options.text: It guarantees that every team member will have the same internet connection speed.
options.explanation: Network speed is unrelated to Notion’s functionality.

questions.question: Which Notion view is most suitable for a client‑facing deadline calendar?

options.text: Board view
options.explanation: Board view is great for Kanban style status tracking, not for calendar‑style deadline visibility.

options.text: Calendar view
options.explanation: Correct. The Calendar layout lets clients see deadlines and deliverables on a shared calendar.

options.text: Gallery view
options.explanation: Gallery view displays items as cards, which isn’t ideal for date‑based tracking.

options.text: Chart view
options.explanation: Chart view visualizes data trends, not individual deadline dates.

questions.question: How can you ensure that new entries in a project tracker come with pre‑filled properties and an outline?

options.text: By creating a database template for the entry type.
options.explanation: Correct. Database templates automatically fill out selected properties and provide a ready‑made outline.

options.text: By manually typing the information each time.
options.explanation: Manual entry defeats the purpose of templates and is time‑consuming.

options.text: By using the Table view only.
options.explanation: Views affect display, not how properties are pre‑filled.

options.text: By enabling the AI auto‑complete feature in the settings.
options.explanation: AI can draft content, but templates are the specific tool for pre‑filled properties.

questions.question: Which property type should you use to group projects by the team member responsible for them?

options.text: Select property
options.explanation: Select is for categorical choices, not for assigning people.

options.text: Date property
options.explanation: Date tracks timelines, not assignees.

options.text: Person property
options.explanation: Correct. Person properties let you assign a user, enabling grouping by assignee.

options.text: Number property
options.explanation: Number is for numeric data, not for people.

questions.question: What is the benefit of using drag‑and‑drop on a Kanban board within Notion?

options.text: It automatically creates a new database entry for each drag.
options.explanation: Dragging moves existing items; it doesn’t generate new entries.

options.text: It updates the status of a task by moving it between columns.
options.explanation: Correct. Moving a card between status columns instantly changes its Status property.

options.text: It changes the view type from Board to Timeline.
options.explanation: Drag‑and‑drop affects item placement, not the overall view layout.

options.text: It synchronizes the task with external project management tools.
options.explanation: Synchronization requires integrations or automations, not just drag‑and‑drop.

questions.question: How can you embed a Slack message directly into a Notion project page?

options.text: By uploading a screenshot of the Slack message.
options.explanation: A screenshot is a static image, not an embedded live Slack message.

options.text: By using the Embed block and pasting the Slack message URL.
options.explanation: Correct. Notion’s Embed block supports Slack URLs, allowing live embeds.

options.text: By converting the Slack message to a PDF and attaching it.
options.explanation: PDF attachment works but isn’t an embed; it doesn’t display the message inline.

options.text: By linking the Slack workspace in the page settings.
options.explanation: Linking the workspace doesn’t embed specific messages.

questions.question: Why would you create multiple saved views for a single project tracker database?

options.text: To permanently lock the database layout so it can’t be changed.
options.explanation: Saved views don’t lock layouts; they allow quick switching between configurations.

options.text: To provide different stakeholders with custom perspectives—like a calendar for clients and a board for the team.
options.explanation: Correct. Saved views let you tailor how data is displayed for various audiences.

options.text: To duplicate the data into separate databases automatically.
options.explanation: Views are just visual filters; they don’t duplicate data.

options.text: To enable offline editing of the database.
options.explanation: Offline editing is a separate feature, not a function of saved views.

questions.question: How can database automations be used to notify team members when a project's status changes?

options.text: By setting up an automation that triggers a Slack or email notification whenever the Status property is updated.
options.explanation: Correct. Notion’s automation can watch property changes and send notifications to designated users.

options.text: By manually sending a message each time you notice a status change.
options.explanation: Manual messaging defeats the purpose of automation.

options.text: By changing the view type to Timeline, which automatically emails the team.
options.explanation: Changing the view does not trigger notifications.

options.text: By using the AI assistant to draft a notification after the status change.
options.explanation: AI can help draft content, but the automation itself is what sends the notification.

questions.question: In what ways can Notion AI enhance the workflow within a project tracker?

options.text: It can automatically assign tasks to the most qualified team member without any input.
options.explanation: AI currently assists with drafting and summarizing, not autonomous task assignment.

options.text: It can draft project briefs, summarize meeting notes, and extract insights from past campaigns to speed up content creation.
options.explanation: Correct. Notion AI helps generate documents, summarize information, and pull insights, improving efficiency.

options.text: It replaces the need for any human review of project deliverables.
options.explanation: Human review remains essential; AI is a supportive tool, not a replacement.

options.text: It converts all database properties into visual charts automatically.
options.explanation: Chart creation still requires manual configuration; AI does not auto‑convert properties.

questions.question: What is the purpose of using a Relation property between client projects and meeting notes?

options.text: To duplicate the meeting notes inside each project page.
options.explanation: Relation links, not duplicates, preserving a single source of truth.

options.text: To create a live connection so that updates in meeting notes are reflected in the linked project.
options.explanation: Correct. Relation properties link databases, ensuring context and up‑to‑date information across pages.

options.text: To automatically generate a new project when a meeting note is created.
options.explanation: Relations do not auto‑create new entries; they just link existing ones.

options.text: To hide the meeting notes from all users except the project owner.
options.explanation: Visibility is controlled by permissions, not by relations.
